items.description: Products are delivered with secure default configurations, and the most secure state is the default.
items.evidence: Configuration hardening guide, default settings audit

items.description: Products protect the confidentiality of stored, transmitted, or processed data.
items.evidence: Encryption at rest and in transit documentation

items.description: Products protect the integrity of stored, transmitted, or processed data.
Products protect the availability of functions critical to the health and safety of users.
Products are designed to protect against unauthorized access by appropriate control mechanisms.
Products protect the confidentiality of authentication credentials.
Products are designed to limit attack surfaces, including external interfaces.
Products are designed to reduce the impact of security incidents.
Products do not contain known exploitable vulnerabilities at time of placement on the market.
Security updates are provided to address vulnerabilities in a timely manner.
Identify and document vulnerabilities and components contained in the product.
Address and remediate vulnerabilities without delay.
Apply regular and automatic security updates and patches.
Disclose information about fixed vulnerabilities to users in a timely manner.
Put in place and enforce a coordinated vulnerability disclosure policy.
Facilitate user understanding of security risks and safe product use.
Ensure that third-party components are covered by the vulnerability management process.

annex_iii.title: Annex III — Technical Documentation and CE Marking

items.description: Technical documentation demonstrates conformity with Annex I and II requirements.
CE marking is affixed visibly, legibly, and indelibly before placement on the market.
EU Declaration of Conformity is drawn up and kept available.
